tools.name: list_datasets
tools.description: List the available hand-verified German municipal tax/fee datasets (Hundesteuer, Zweitwohnungsteuer, Pfändung), their coverage, source sites and refresh cadence. Call this first to see what you can query.

tools.name: hundesteuer_lookup
tools.description: Look up the German dog tax (Hundesteuer) for a city: annual rate for one or more dogs, listed-dog (Listenhund) surcharge, the city's pricing model, its rank among the largest cities, plus the official source and Stand. Rates verified against each municipal Satzung.

tools.name: hundesteuer_ranking
tools.description: Ranking of German cities by Hundesteuer (first dog, per year): most expensive or cheapest, plus the national average across the covered cities.

tools.name: hundesteuer_by_state
tools.description: Average German dog tax (Hundesteuer, first dog per year) per Bundesland (federal state), aggregated over the covered largest cities — the data behind the Deutschlandkarte. Answers 'which German state has the highest/lowest dog tax'. Note: a mean over cities, not an official state rate (the tax is mu

tools.name: zweitwohnungsteuer_lookup
tools.description: Look up the German second-home tax (Zweitwohnungsteuer) for a city: whether the city levies it, the rate and its basis (net cold rent etc.), the rank among cities, official source and Stand. Optionally pass a monthly cold rent to get the annual tax. Returns state = keine_steuer | berechenbar | sonde

tools.name: zweitwohnungsteuer_ranking
tools.description: Ranking of German cities by Zweitwohnungsteuer rate: highest or lowest, plus the average, count with a computable rate, and count of cities with no second-home tax.

tools.name: zweitwohnungsteuer_changes
tools.description: List German cities that changed (raised or newly introduced) their Zweitwohnungsteuer in a given year — the fresh-news angle. Defaults to 2026 (Stuttgart, Chemnitz, Heidelberg doubled to 20%, etc.).

tools.name: zweitwohnungsteuer_by_state
tools.description: Average German second-home tax (Zweitwohnungsteuer) rate in percent per Bundesland (federal state), aggregated over the levying cities with a computable rate — the data behind the Deutschlandkarte. Answers 'which German state has the highest/lowest second-home tax'. Note: a mean over cities, not an

tools.name: pfaendung_calc
tools.description: Compute the attachable portion of monthly net income under German §850c ZPO (Pfändung / wage garnishment), using the current official Pfändungsfreigrenzenbekanntmachung. Returns attachable amount, remaining amount, exempt base and the P-Konto base. Informational, not legal advice.
